sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), created_at VARCHAR(19));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at) VALUES (1, 'Alice', '2020-09-28 18:40:05');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at) VALUES (2, 'Bob', '2020-09-28 18:40:06');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at) VALUES (3, NULL, '2020-09-28 18:40:07');
SELECT CAST(name AS VARCHAR(20)), timestamp(CAST(created_at AS VARCHAR(19))) FROM test_table WHERE id IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
SELECT id, CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR(10)) FROM test_table GROUP BY id ORDER BY CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR(10));
SELECT t1.id, CAST(t1.name AS VARCHAR(30)) FROM test_table t1 JOIN test_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t2.name IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE another_table (a INT, b VARCHAR(30), c TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO another_table (a, b, c) VALUES (1, '2022-02-02 12:00:00', timestamp '2022-02-02 12:00:00');
SELECT a, CAST(b AS VARCHAR(5)) AS b_str, CAST(c AS VARCHAR(30)) FROM another_table WHERE CAST(c AS TIMESTAMP) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, AVG(CAST(CAST(created_at AS VARCHAR(19)) AS TIMESTAMP)) FROM test_table GROUP BY id ORDER BY 1;
SELECT t1.id, t1.name FROM test_table t1 LEFT JOIN another_table t2 ON CAST(t1.created_at AS TIMESTAMP) = t2.c WHERE t2.b IS NOT NULL;
SELECT CAST(NULL AS VARCHAR(10)), COUNT(NULL) FROM test_table;
DELETE FROM test_table WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table);
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD COLUMN description VARCHAR(100);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at, description) VALUES (4, 'Charlie', '2021-01-01 00:00:00', 'This is a description.');
SELECT id, description FROM test_table WHERE description IS NOT NULL;
